posts.text:
𝗦𝘁𝗼𝗿𝗺 𝗨𝗽𝗱𝗮𝘁𝗲:

I’ve been in touch with local leadership throughout the state to make sure jurisdictions are getting what they need to respond to storm damage.

Thank you to @MDMEMA, all of our emergency response crews, law enforcement partners for their work, and the people of Maryland for heeding warnings and being prepared.

I have declared a State of Preparedness ahead of significant risk for widespread, severe storms and a heightened tornado threat across the state tomorrow, with @MDMEMA coordinating readiness efforts across state government.

The safety of Maryland families comes first, and I am urging everyone to prepare early, stay informed, and adjust plans – including postponing travel – as conditions change.

Please review preparedness recommendations at go.md.gov/StormPrep
Spent the other day talking with some of our world’s future leaders at Fort Washington Forest Elementary School!

Every time our nation has been tested, Maryland has answered the challenge with strength.

In this moment too, we will hold the line and build the kind of future that those who came before us fought for – and those who come after us deserve.

Video
Honored to have met Grammy Award winners Aura V – the youngest award winner in history – and her incredible and talented father Fyütch!

We will not let the failures by regional operators like PJM define how Maryland moves at this moment. 

Marylanders deserve affordable energy bills. Full stop. 

Together with Senate President Ferguson and Speaker Peña-Melnyk, we will hold utility companies accountable, move faster to build new energy generation, and deliver $100 million to lower energy bills for Maryland families.

Video

Senate President Bill Ferguson, House Speaker Joseline Peña-Melnyk, and I have announced a comprehensive legislative package to lower energy bills for Marylanders. 

The legislation will help our state generate more energy, hold big utility companies accountable, and – importantly – 𝙨𝙖𝙫𝙚 𝙈𝙖𝙧𝙮𝙡𝙖𝙣𝙙 𝙛𝙖𝙢𝙞𝙡𝙞𝙚𝙨 𝙝𝙪𝙣𝙙𝙧𝙚𝙙𝙨 𝙤𝙣 𝙩𝙝𝙚𝙞𝙧 𝙚𝙣𝙚𝙧𝙜𝙮 𝙗𝙞𝙡𝙡𝙨 𝙚𝙫𝙚𝙧𝙮 𝙮𝙚𝙖𝙧.

Today, Maryland breathes a prayer of relief that Tristan King has been found after months of fear, heartbreak, and uncertainty. 

The first priority now is making sure Tristan is safe, supported, and surrounded by the care he needs to begin healing. We are deeply grateful to everyone who helped bring him home — including law enforcement, child welfare professionals, community members, and every person who refused to stop looking for him. 

We owe Tristan action, not excuses, and we will keep wor

I am devastated to learn of yet another attack on a synagogue. At a time when Jewish communities across our country are faced with rising antisemitism, an attack on a house of worship is an attack on the very heart of our nation.

My prayers are with the congregants of Temple Israel and with the entire Michigan Jewish community. Maryland stands in solidarity with you, as we reaffirm that hate has no place in our communities, and we must and we will always speak out against antisemitism wherever

Thank you again to Brother Hammad and everyone at the Islamic Society of Baltimore for hosting @LtGovMiller and I for iftar last night.

A recent report shows that just last year, there were thousands of complaints of anti-Muslim discrimination, the highest number in 30 years. In the last few weeks, we’ve even seen members of Congress post disgusting anti-Muslim hatred. It’s outrageous. 

As Governor, there is no higher priority than the safety of our people. Muslims belong in our country, Musli

UPDATE: This is a welcome step toward protecting our state’s environment, economic interests, and local infrastructure, while upholding human dignity and the rule of law.




Governor Wes Moore (@GovWesMoore)

You can’t go around terrorizing communities and hurting people and think that’s okay. Not in Maryland. 

That’s why @OAGBrown and I are suing to help protect Marylanders from the Trump administration’s ICE operation. 

No se puede andar aterrorizando comunidades y lastimando a la gente y p

The allegations surrounding the conditions at the George H. Fallon Federal Building in Baltimore are deeply disturbing and merit full investigative cooperation. We cannot stand by as ICE and the federal government continue to dodge accountability for their cruel and unlawful immigration enforcement actions.

In Maryland, we uphold the Constitution. We recognize our solemn obligation to promote public safety and defend civil rights, and we will pursue justice to protect our people.

It’s always great being in the room with Maryland’s police chiefs and law enforcement community because we all hold the same belief: That our people deserve to be safe in their communities.

Our partnership is essential to the state’s success. Homicides are down nearly 50% statewide and our budget proposes a record $124.1 million for the State Aid for Police Protection program to keep up our progress.

Dawn and I are heartbroken by former Lt. Governor Mickey Steinberg’s passing this morning.

From his service in the Navy to his tenure as Lt. Governor and beyond, he led a life of public service and deep love for Maryland. His leadership, including during the savings and loan crisis, and his work as Senate President made our state a better place to live and work.

Our prayers are with family and friends.
